hypothesis: If a convolutional neural network is first pretrained on a data-rich domain of additive-free SEM images before being fine-tuned on a data-poor domain of additive-containing samples, then the resulting transfer learning model will achieve significantly higher classification accuracy for NCM cathode composition and aging state identification compared to models trained without pretraining, because the pretrained feature representations capture morphological patterns common to both domains that compensate for the scarcity of labeled target-domain data.
